pages.raw_text:
The most notable event of the
day tomorrow will be the dance
given at Fort Wright. The hops
at the Fort have been among the
smartest affairs of the season and
the affair tomorrow will be carried
out with no less of the military
brilliance than on former occa-
sions. About $50 invitations have
been issued to the members or the
society set.


The members of the Northwest
Literary club will meet tomorrow
afternoon with Mrs. Woodlock at
the Woodlock residence on Boone
avenue. The subject will be Ten
nyson,' and a paper will be read
by Mrs. Homer Frost.


4 4 4


The domestic science department
of the Woman's club will meet with
their teacher, Miss Anna Rogers,
at $50 o'clock tomorrow afternoon
at the North Central high school.
The subject of the lecture Will be
Cooking of Carbohydrates, Fats
and heterogeneous Foods"


4 &


Mrs. C. r. Howard will entertain
the chapter of the e. p. r. o. We'd
nesday at her home on Boone ave-
nue..


Circle No. 6, Ladies' Aid society
of the First Methodist church will
meet Wednesday afternoon at z
o'clock with Mrs. r. w. Crosby at
her home, 40S Knox avenue.


4 4 4


Mrs. II. A. Chisholm will enter
tain the members of the Thistle
club tomorrow afternoon at
o'clock at her residence on Knox
avenue.


In celebration of the birthday oil
Robert e. Lee. DR.. and Mrs. L. A.
Robinson will entertain the mem
bers of the Mildred Lee chapter
Daughters of the Confederacy, at
their homo on Jerome avenue.


Mrs. J. H. Bell will entertain the
st. James Guild of Mantle park to-
morrow afternoon.


=


The members of the Young Wo
mems Christian association will
hold n business meeting tomorrow
morning for the election of officers
for the coming year. The retiring
officers are Mrs. J. w. Simpson
president; Mrs. j. b. Campbell
first vice president: Mrs. II. G
Blair, secretary; Mrs. A. w. Do
land, treasurer
